text: Maps are quiet acts of translation, turning the vastness of the world into something a hand can hold and an eye can cross. Every map is also an argument: what to include, what to omit, where to place the center. We forget how much they leave out, mistaking the clean line of a coast for the ragged truth of waves and rock. And yet there is tenderness in them, in the wish to make the unknown legible, to say here is where you are, and here is how you might find your way home.
